hero.title:
I build agents,
their tools,
and the tools around the tools.
hero.sub: The work starts as a conversation, turns into a prototype, and ends as software with a shape you can keep.
hero.note: I like hard problems with human texture: messy product edges, strange operational constraints, and ideas that need form.
points: Agent strategy translated into working software
Rapid prototypes that teach something real
Architecture with enough discipline to last

positioning.title: Notes from the workshop
positioning.intro: Independent systems engineering, especially when the problem refuses to stay inside one box. I work across product language, system design, and implementation until the thing is legible.

items.title: Agent design
items.body: Tool boundaries, prompt surfaces, evaluation loops, and the operating model around them.

items.title: Prototype direction
items.body: Fast builds that answer a real question instead of acting busy.

items.title: Production shape
items.body: Deep modules, cleaner interfaces, fewer moving parts, and no ornamental complexity.

positioning.quote: Build the real thing early enough to learn from it.

contact.title: Bring me the knotty version
contact.intro: If the brief is ambiguous, the system is new, or the software needs taste as much as speed, that is usually the right starting point.

hero.title:
Independent engineering
for agents,
tooling, and infrastructure.
hero.sub: Rapid prototyping on request. Systems review when the stakes are higher than the org chart suggests.
hero.note: Useful when a team needs a sharp external operator: spec the work, build the hard slice, leave the system cleaner than it started.
points: Legacy modernization sprints with a delivery bias

positioning.title: Program lines
positioning.intro: The job is to compress decision time without lowering the bar. I join the project where ambiguity, velocity, and technical depth collide.

items.title: Autonomous systems
items.body: Agent loops, tool calling, review councils, and task routing.

items.title: Rapid prototypes
items.body: Build the operational slice first, prove the path, then widen the surface area.

items.title: Systems cleanup
items.body: Refactor the sharp edges, remove shallow glue, and leave behind a simpler interface.

positioning.quote: Agents, tooling, infrastructure. Buildable now, maintainable later.

contact.title: Need a fast systems pass?
contact.intro: I take scoped problems, unblock critical paths, and deliver the piece that lets the rest of the team move again.

hero.title:
I build agents,
their infrastructure,
and the tools around them.
hero.sub: Independent systems engineering for teams that need working prototypes, reliable harnesses, and software that survives first contact.
hero.note: Rapid prototyping when the idea is still soft. Hard engineering when it needs to hold.

positioning.title: Agent architect. Independent systems engineering.
positioning.intro: I work where product, infrastructure, and agent behavior meet. The point is not a proof of concept. The point is a system with a clear shape and a short path to shipping.

items.title: Agent systems
items.body: Multi-agent workflows, tool surfaces, evaluation, and control loops.

items.title: Tooling and harnesses
items.body: CLIs, operator consoles, review automation, and the infrastructure around agents.

items.title: Rapid prototyping
items.body: Tight cycles from zero to credible software, then the cleanup that keeps it from collapsing.

positioning.quote: I build agents, the tools to build agents, and the tools to build the tools.

contact.title: Bring me the sharp edge
contact.intro: New product, stubborn legacy system, agent infra that needs discipline, or a prototype that has to exist next week. That is the lane.
